options: Survive configuration changes and hold UI-related data

hard.q: What is the difference between `launch` and `async` in Kotlin Coroutines?
options: `launch` returns a Deferred; `async` returns a Job
`launch` returns a Job (fire-and-forget); `async` returns a Deferred (with result)

hard.q: In WorkManager, which constraint ensures work only runs when connected to a network?

Marks a function for dependency injection
Marks a function as a ViewHolder
Marks a function that describes a piece of UI

hard.q: Which pattern does Android's Room library use for database access?

options: Rebuilding the entire app from scratch
Re-executing composable functions when observed state changes

easy.q: What is the primary programming language for iOS development?
What does UIKit provide?
options: UI components and infrastructure for building iOS apps

easy.q: Which method is called first in a UIViewController's lifecycle?
What is SwiftUI?
options: A framework for building UI declaratively with Swift

easy.q: What is a Storyboard in iOS development?
options: A visual tool to design and connect UI screens

medium.q: What is ARC in iOS?
options: Automatic Reference Counting — manages memory by tracking object references

medium.q: Which SwiftUI property wrapper marks a variable as a local source of truth?
What is the Combine framework used for?
options: Processing values over time using publishers and subscribers

medium.q: Why are `weak` references used in Swift?
options: To avoid strong reference cycles that cause memory leaks

medium.q: Which class is used to make network requests in iOS?

hard.q: How do `struct` and `class` differ in Swift regarding memory storage?
options: Classes are value types; structs are reference types
Structs are value types on the stack; classes are reference types on the heap

hard.q: What does `@EnvironmentObject` do in SwiftUI?
options: Creates a new object instance per view
Injects a shared observable object into the view hierarchy without passing it explicitly

hard.q: What is a 'strong reference cycle' in Swift?
options: A cycle in a linked list
When two objects hold strong references to each other, preventing deallocation

hard.q: Which GCD queue must always be used for UI updates?
What does `@discardableResult` do in Swift?
options: Marks a function that throws errors
Suppresses the compiler warning when a function's return value is not used

easy.q: What programming language does Flutter use?
What is the fundamental building block of a Flutter UI?
Which widget arranges children vertically in Flutter?
What does Hot Reload do in Flutter?
options: Rebuilds the widget tree from scratch
Injects updated code into the running app without losing state

easy.q: What is the CLI command to create a new Flutter project?

medium.q: What is the key difference between StatelessWidget and StatefulWidget?
options: StatelessWidget can rebuild itself; StatefulWidget cannot
StatelessWidget is faster but not recommended
StatefulWidget holds mutable state that can trigger rebuilds; StatelessWidget does not

medium.q: Which method must every State class implement in Flutter?
What is a BuildContext in Flutter?
options: A handle to the widget's location in the widget tree

medium.q: What does FutureBuilder do in Flutter?
options: Builds a widget subtree based on the latest snapshot of a Future

medium.q: Which package is a widely used state management solution in Flutter?

hard.q: What is the purpose of the `key` property in Flutter widgets?
options: A unique ID for debugging only
Required for all widgets to render
Helps Flutter efficiently identify and update specific widgets during reconciliation

hard.q: What is a Dart Isolate?
options: A way to isolate network calls
An independent worker with its own memory heap for concurrent execution

hard.q: What does InheritedWidget do in Flutter?
options: Extends the lifecycle of a StatefulWidget
Efficiently propagates data down the widget tree without passing through constructors

hard.q: What is the render tree in Flutter?
options: A list of all visible widgets
A layer below the element tree that handles actual pixel painting

hard.q: What is LayoutBuilder used for in Flutter?
options: Managing widget layouts at compile time
Building widgets that depend on the parent's size constraints

easy.q: What language is used to write React Native apps?
Which component displays scrollable lists efficiently in React Native?
What does React Native use instead of HTML divs for layout?
Which React hook manages local state in a component?
What is the Metro bundler?
options: A JavaScript bundler that transforms and serves React Native code

medium.q: What was the 'Bridge' in the classic React Native architecture?
options: The async communication layer between JavaScript and native code

medium.q: What is the main performance difference between FlatList and ScrollView?
options: ScrollView renders all children at once; FlatList renders lazily for better performance

medium.q: What does the useEffect hook do in React Native?
options: Runs side effects like data fetching after render

medium.q: Which library is the most popular for navigation in React Native?
What does the Platform module allow you to do?
options: Detect the OS and write platform-specific conditional logic

hard.q: What is JSI (JavaScript Interface) in React Native's New Architecture?
options: A new version of the Bridge
A C++ layer that lets JS call native functions synchronously without serialization

hard.q: What is Fabric in React Native's New Architecture?
options: The new UI rendering system that synchronously connects native views with the JS thread

hard.q: What is the purpose of useCallback in React Native?
options: To memoize a callback, preventing unnecessary re-creation on every re-render

hard.q: How does Hermes improve React Native performance?
options: By pre-compiling JS to bytecode, reducing startup time and memory usage

hard.q: What are TurboModules in React Native's New Architecture?
options: An optimized native module system using JSI for lazy loading of native modules

easy.q: What programming language is used in Xamarin development?

options: Deploy apps to the App Store only
Write shared UI code that runs on iOS, Android, and other platforms

easy.q: What is XAML in Xamarin?
options: A markup language used to define UIs declaratively

easy.q: Which Microsoft product has Xamarin evolved into?
What is ContentPage in Xamarin.Forms?
options: A page type that displays a single view as its content

medium.q: What is data binding in Xamarin.Forms?
options: Automatically syncing UI elements with data sources

medium.q: Which design pattern is most commonly used with Xamarin.Forms?
What is ICommand used for in Xamarin MVVM?
options: Binding button actions to ViewModel logic

medium.q: What is the Dependency Service in Xamarin.Forms?
options: A mechanism to inject platform-specific implementations into shared code

medium.q: What does ObservableCollection<T> provide in Xamarin?
options: A collection that notifies the UI when items are added, removed, or changed

hard.q: What is a Custom Renderer in Xamarin.Forms?
options: A platform-specific class that overrides the default rendering of a Xamarin.Forms control

hard.q: What is Shell navigation in Xamarin.Forms?
options: A way to navigate between projects
A URI-based navigation system with built-in flyout and tab bar support

hard.q: What is the purpose of Effects in Xamarin.Forms?
options: Lightweight platform customizations to a control without a full Custom Renderer

hard.q: How does MessagingCenter work in Xamarin.Forms?
options: A pub-sub system for loosely coupled communication between components

hard.q: What is the difference between BindingContext and DataContext?
options: BindingContext is Xamarin.Forms specific; DataContext is from WPF / .NET MAUI

easy.q: What core web technologies does Ionic use?

options: A native runtime that bridges web code to native device APIs

easy.q: Which Ionic component displays a list?
What does ion-router-outlet do?
options: Acts as a placeholder where routed page components are rendered

easy.q: Which framework does Ionic most commonly pair with?

medium.q: What is the key difference between Cordova and Capacitor?
options: Cordova is maintained by the Ionic team
Cordova is the legacy native bridge; Capacitor is its modern, web-first replacement

medium.q: Which Ionic lifecycle hook fires every time a page becomes the active view?
What is Ionic Storage used for?
options: A key-value store abstracting LocalStorage, IndexedDB, and SQLite

medium.q: How does Ionic handle platform-specific styling automatically?
options: CSS variables + automatically applying MD or iOS mode based on the detected platform

medium.q: What is ion-infinite-scroll used for?
options: Loading more data as the user scrolls to the bottom of a list

hard.q: How is a Capacitor Plugin structured?
options: A web implementation + platform-specific native implementations for iOS and Android

hard.q: What does the @capacitor/app backButton event handle?
options: Creating custom back navigation in iOS
The Android hardware back button press in JavaScript

hard.q: What is the difference between ActivatedRoute and Router in Ionic Angular?
options: Router is only for lazy loading
ActivatedRoute gives info about the current route; Router navigates programmatically

hard.q: What does lazy loading do in Ionic Angular?
options: Loads feature modules only when their route is first accessed, reducing bundle size

hard.q: How does ionViewDidEnter differ from ngAfterViewInit?
options: They fire at the same time
ngAfterViewInit replaces all Ionic lifecycle hooks
ngAfterViewInit fires once on creation; ionViewDidEnter fires every time the page enters the viewport
